matches.moment: Mbappé and Dembélé combine in transition to breach Spain's high defensive line before Mikel Merino, introduced from the bench, heads Spain back into it late, extending his tournament-best record as a super-sub.

verdict: This is the cleanest attack-versus-defense collision the tournament has produced: France own the highest aggregate xG of any side in the field by a margin of 1.6, built on the highest shots-on-target rate since 1966, while Spain have built a defense that has conceded the fewest shots on target and the lowest xG per shot faced of any team in the same window. Neither side is a pretender; both reached this stage as the pre-tournament co-favorites, and the numbers back that billing up. The data does not support a France procession, despite the attacking gap. Spain's defense is not merely good, it is statistically unprecedented at this level, and their recent head-to-head record against these exact opponents, decided by Lamine Yamal in each of the last two competitive meetings, is a genuine caution against assuming France's volume translates cleanly into a comfortable scoreline. What tips this the way of Les Bleus is the qualitative pattern sitting underneath the aggregate numbers: France do not need sustained pressure to score, they need one crack in a defensive structure, and their front three have shown all tournament that once that crack appears, goals follow in bursts. Spain's own goals, by contrast, have arrived late and via a substitute in each of their last two knockout wins, a sign of a team that grinds rather than carves opponents open, which will make life hard against a France backline that has already won 62 percent of its ground duels this summer. The winner of this one plays for the World Cup on July 19 at MetLife Stadium, and on the balance of the numbers, that team is more likely to be wearing blue than red, but only just.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.watch: A moment of individual brilliance to break the deadlock, his tiebreaker vs Morocco is the template

players.name: Michael Olise
players.stat: xG+xA 4.5; 5 assists (tournament co-lead), 0 goals from 2.1 xG
players.watch: Whether he finally converts one of his own chances rather than only creating for others

players.name: Lamine Yamal
players.stat: xG+xA 2.5; 1 goal, 0 assists despite tournament-leading 78 duels attempted and 36 won
players.watch: Whether Spain can finally unlock his end product against France, as he did in each of the last two meetings

players.name: Mikel Merino
players.stat: xG+xA 1.8; 2 goals, both as a substitute after the 85th minute
players.watch: A repeat impact substitute appearance if the game remains tight into the final 15 minutes
